revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Stub category/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Stub category/doc]]</includeonly>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:Stub category/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Stub category/doc]]</includeonly>

#redirect[[:en:Template:Stub category/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:Stub category/doc]]</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use|15376}} {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|The {{tlx|regional stub category}} template operates identically to {{tlx|stub category}} except that it also categorises the page into {{cl|Stubs by region}}.}} __FORCETOC__ ==Usage== {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article= |newstub= |category= |category type= |WikiProject= |message= }} For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message= }} will show: {{Stub category|article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc.|newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub}} The {{para|category type}} parameter inserts additional text between the phrases "This category is for" and "[[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...", so using {{para|category type|biographical}} will give "This category is for biographical [[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...". === Optional parameters === ==== WikiProject ==== {{para|WikiProject}} links to a relevant WikiProject page. For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]] |newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category=Massachusetts |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts }} will show: {{stub category|article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]]|newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts}} ==== message ==== {{para|message}} adds a message displayed below the main output of the template, but above the table of contents (TOC) which will be automatically generated by {{tl|Automatic category TOC}} if the category contains enough pages. This ensures that the message doesn't get tangled up in the TOC. {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message=This category is only people involved in [[association football]] (soccer). Please do NOT use this stub tag for players of other types of football, e.g. [[Gaelic football]], [[Australian rules football]], [[rugby union]]. }} ===Tracking categories=== Certain cases of incorrect usage are tracked: *the {{para|newstub}} parameter is blank or absent; *the {{para|category}} parameter is blank or absent. See [[:Category:Stub categories needing attention]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories needing attention}}) Usage of the {{param|message}} parameter is also tracked.
See [[:Category:Stub categories with message parameter]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories with message parameter}}) {{Purge|''Purge this page to update the totals''}} ==== Fixing P-type categories ==== These are categories that lack a 'primary category' that is not a stub category. To fix this, add the primary category to the "category" parameter in the template; this will place the category into the newly specified parent category and remove the category from the "P" subsection in the "Stub categories needing attention" category. Simply adding the new category to the existing list of categories will properly categorize the category, but it will not remove the category from the "attention" category. ==See also== {{other category-header templates}} {{Sandbox other|| {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|| [[Category:Stub categories| {{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Category namespace templates]] [[Category:WikiProject Stub sorting templates]] [[ro:Format:CiotCat]] }}}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use|15376}} {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|The {{tlx|regional stub category}} template operates identically to {{tlx|stub category}} except that it also categorises the page into {{cl|Stubs by region}}.}} __FORCETOC__ ==Usage== {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article= |newstub= |category= |category type= |WikiProject= |message= }} For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message= }} will show: {{Stub category|article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc.|newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub}} The {{para|category type}} parameter inserts additional text between the phrases "This category is for" and "[[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...", so using {{para|category type|biographical}} will give "This category is for biographical [[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...". === Optional parameters === ==== WikiProject ==== {{para|WikiProject}} links to a relevant WikiProject page. For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]] |newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category=Massachusetts |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts }} will show: {{stub category|article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]]|newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts}} ==== message ==== {{para|message}} adds a message displayed below the main output of the template, but above the table of contents (TOC) which will be automatically generated by {{tl|Automatic category TOC}} if the category contains enough pages. This ensures that the message doesn't get tangled up in the TOC. {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message=This category is only people involved in [[:en:Association football|Association football]] (soccer). Please do NOT use this stub tag for players of other types of football, e.g. [[Gaelic football]], [[Australian rules football]], [[rugby union]]. }} ===Tracking categories=== Certain cases of incorrect usage are tracked: *the {{para|newstub}} parameter is blank or absent; *the {{para|category}} parameter is blank or absent. See [[:Category:Stub categories needing attention]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories needing attention}}) Usage of the {{param|message}} parameter is also tracked.
See [[:Category:Stub categories with message parameter]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories with message parameter}}) {{Purge|''Purge this page to update the totals''}} ==== Fixing P-type categories ==== These are categories that lack a 'primary category' that is not a stub category. To fix this, add the primary category to the "category" parameter in the template; this will place the category into the newly specified parent category and remove the category from the "P" subsection in the "Stub categories needing attention" category. Simply adding the new category to the existing list of categories will properly categorize the category, but it will not remove the category from the "attention" category. ==See also== {{other category-header templates}} {{Sandbox other|| {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|| [[Category:Stub categories| {{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Category namespace templates]] [[Category:WikiProject Stub sorting templates]] [[ro:Format:CiotCat]] }}}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use|15376}} {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|The {{tlx|regional stub category}} template operates identically to {{tlx|stub category}} except that it also categorises the page into {{cl|Stubs by region}}.}} __FORCETOC__ ==Usage== {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article= |newstub= |category= |category type= |WikiProject= |message= }} For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message= }} will show: {{Stub category|article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc.|newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub}} The {{para|category type}} parameter inserts additional text between the phrases "This category is for" and "[[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...", so using {{para|category type|biographical}} will give "This category is for biographical [[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...". === Optional parameters === ==== WikiProject ==== {{para|WikiProject}} links to a relevant WikiProject page. For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]] |newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category=Massachusetts |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts }} will show: {{stub category|article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]]|newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts}} ==== message ==== {{para|message}} adds a message displayed below the main output of the template, but above the table of contents (TOC) which will be automatically generated by {{tl|Automatic category TOC}} if the category contains enough pages. This ensures that the message doesn't get tangled up in the TOC. {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message=This category is only people involved in [[:en:Association football|Association football]] (soccer). Please do NOT use this stub tag for players of other types of football, e.g. [[Gaelic football]], [[:en:Australian rules football|Australian rules football]], [[rugby union]]. }} ===Tracking categories=== Certain cases of incorrect usage are tracked: *the {{para|newstub}} parameter is blank or absent; *the {{para|category}} parameter is blank or absent. See [[:Category:Stub categories needing attention]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories needing attention}}) Usage of the {{param|message}} parameter is also tracked.
See [[:Category:Stub categories with message parameter]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories with message parameter}}) {{Purge|''Purge this page to update the totals''}} ==== Fixing P-type categories ==== These are categories that lack a 'primary category' that is not a stub category. To fix this, add the primary category to the "category" parameter in the template; this will place the category into the newly specified parent category and remove the category from the "P" subsection in the "Stub categories needing attention" category. Simply adding the new category to the existing list of categories will properly categorize the category, but it will not remove the category from the "attention" category. ==See also== {{other category-header templates}} {{Sandbox other|| {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|| [[Category:Stub categories| {{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Category namespace templates]] [[Category:WikiProject Stub sorting templates]] [[ro:Format:CiotCat]] }}}}

revision.comment: Bot: tag as missing wikidata (no wikidata found via interwikis)
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use|15376}} {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|The {{tlx|regional stub category}} template operates identically to {{tlx|stub category}} except that it also categorises the page into {{cl|Stubs by region}}.}} __FORCETOC__ ==Usage== {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article= |newstub= |category= |category type= |WikiProject= |message= }} For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message= }} will show: {{Stub category|article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc.|newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub}} The {{para|category type}} parameter inserts additional text between the phrases "This category is for" and "[[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...", so using {{para|category type|biographical}} will give "This category is for biographical [[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...". === Optional parameters === ==== WikiProject ==== {{para|WikiProject}} links to a relevant WikiProject page. For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]] |newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category=Massachusetts |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts }} will show: {{stub category|article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]]|newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts}} ==== message ==== {{para|message}} adds a message displayed below the main output of the template, but above the table of contents (TOC) which will be automatically generated by {{tl|Automatic category TOC}} if the category contains enough pages. This ensures that the message doesn't get tangled up in the TOC. {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message=This category is only people involved in [[:en:Association football|Association football]] (soccer). Please do NOT use this stub tag for players of other types of football, e.g. [[Gaelic football]], [[:en:Australian rules football|Australian rules football]], [[rugby union]]. }} ===Tracking categories=== Certain cases of incorrect usage are tracked: *the {{para|newstub}} parameter is blank or absent; *the {{para|category}} parameter is blank or absent. See [[:Category:Stub categories needing attention]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories needing attention}}) Usage of the {{param|message}} parameter is also tracked.
See [[:Category:Stub categories with message parameter]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories with message parameter}}) {{Purge|''Purge this page to update the totals''}} ==== Fixing P-type categories ==== These are categories that lack a 'primary category' that is not a stub category. To fix this, add the primary category to the "category" parameter in the template; this will place the category into the newly specified parent category and remove the category from the "P" subsection in the "Stub categories needing attention" category. Simply adding the new category to the existing list of categories will properly categorize the category, but it will not remove the category from the "attention" category. ==See also== {{other category-header templates}} {{Sandbox other|| {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|| [[Category:Stub categories| {{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Category namespace templates]] [[Category:WikiProject Stub sorting templates]] [[ro:Format:CiotCat]] }}}} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use|15376}} {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|The {{tlx|regional stub category}} template operates identically to {{tlx|stub category}} except that it also categorises the page into {{cl|Stubs by region}}.}} __FORCETOC__ ==Usage== {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article= |newstub= |category= |category type= |WikiProject= |message= }} For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message= }} will show: {{Stub category|article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc.|newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub}} The {{para|category type}} parameter inserts additional text between the phrases "This category is for" and "[[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...", so using {{para|category type|biographical}} will give "This category is for biographical [[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...". === Optional parameters === ==== WikiProject ==== {{para|WikiProject}} links to a relevant WikiProject page. For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]] |newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category=Massachusetts |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts }} will show: {{stub category|article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]]|newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts}} ==== message ==== {{para|message}} adds a message displayed below the main output of the template, but above the table of contents (TOC) which will be automatically generated by {{tl|Automatic category TOC}} if the category contains enough pages. This ensures that the message doesn't get tangled up in the TOC. {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message=This category is only people involved in [[:en:Association football|Association football]] (soccer). Please do NOT use this stub tag for players of other types of football, e.g. [[Gaelic football]], [[:en:Australian rules football|Australian rules football]], [[rugby union]]. }} ===Tracking categories=== Certain cases of incorrect usage are tracked: *the {{para|newstub}} parameter is blank or absent; *the {{para|category}} parameter is blank or absent. See [[:Category:Stub categories needing attention]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories needing attention}}) Usage of the {{param|message}} parameter is also tracked.
See [[:Category:Stub categories with message parameter]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories with message parameter}}) {{Purge|''Purge this page to update the totals''}} ==== Fixing P-type categories ==== These are categories that lack a 'primary category' that is not a stub category. To fix this, add the primary category to the "category" parameter in the template; this will place the category into the newly specified parent category and remove the category from the "P" subsection in the "Stub categories needing attention" category. Simply adding the new category to the existing list of categories will properly categorize the category, but it will not remove the category from the "attention" category. ==See also== {{other category-header templates}} {{Sandbox other|| {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|| [[Category:Stub categories| {{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Category namespace templates]] [[Category:WikiProject Stub sorting templates]] [[ro:Format:CiotCat]] }}}}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use|15376}} {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|The {{tlx|regional stub category}} template operates identically to {{tlx|stub category}} except that it also categorises the page into {{cl|Stubs by region}}.}} __FORCETOC__ ==Usage== {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article= |newstub= |category= |category type= |WikiProject= |message= }} For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message= }} will show: {{Stub category|article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc.|newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub}} The {{para|category type}} parameter inserts additional text between the phrases "This category is for" and "[[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...", so using {{para|category type|biographical}} will give "This category is for biographical [[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...". === Optional parameters === ==== WikiProject ==== {{para|WikiProject}} links to a relevant WikiProject page. For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]] |newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category=Massachusetts |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts }} will show: {{stub category|article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]]|newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts}} ==== message ==== {{para|message}} adds a message displayed below the main output of the template, but above the table of contents (TOC) which will be automatically generated by {{tl|Automatic category TOC}} if the category contains enough pages. This ensures that the message doesn't get tangled up in the TOC. {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message=This category is only people involved in [[:en:Association football|Association football]] (soccer). Please do NOT use this stub tag for players of other types of football, e.g. [[Gaelic football]], [[:en:Australian rules football|Australian rules football]], [[rugby union]]. }} ===Tracking categories=== Certain cases of incorrect usage are tracked: *the {{para|newstub}} parameter is blank or absent; *the {{para|category}} parameter is blank or absent. See [[:Category:Stub categories needing attention]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories needing attention}}) Usage of the {{param|message}} parameter is also tracked.
See [[:Category:Stub categories with message parameter]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories with message parameter}}) {{Purge|''Purge this page to update the totals''}} ==== Fixing P-type categories ==== These are categories that lack a 'primary category' that is not a stub category. To fix this, add the primary category to the "category" parameter in the template; this will place the category into the newly specified parent category and remove the category from the "P" subsection in the "Stub categories needing attention" category. Simply adding the new category to the existing list of categories will properly categorize the category, but it will not remove the category from the "attention" category. ==See also== {{other category-header templates}} {{Sandbox other|| {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|| [[Category:Stub categories| {{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Category namespace templates]] [[Category:WikiProject Stub sorting templates]] [[ro:Format:CiotCat]] }}}} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 4 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23606946259|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use|15376}} {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|The {{tlx|regional stub category}} template operates identically to {{tlx|stub category}} except that it also categorises the page into {{cl|Stubs by region}}.}} __FORCETOC__ ==Usage== {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article= |newstub= |category= |category type= |WikiProject= |message= }} For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message= }} will show: {{Stub category|article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc.|newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub}} The {{para|category type}} parameter inserts additional text between the phrases "This category is for" and "[[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...", so using {{para|category type|biographical}} will give "This category is for biographical [[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...". === Optional parameters === ==== WikiProject ==== {{para|WikiProject}} links to a relevant WikiProject page. For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]] |newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category=Massachusetts |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts }} will show: {{stub category|article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]]|newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts}} ==== message ==== {{para|message}} adds a message displayed below the main output of the template, but above the table of contents (TOC) which will be automatically generated by {{tl|Automatic category TOC}} if the category contains enough pages. This ensures that the message doesn't get tangled up in the TOC. {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message=This category is only people involved in [[:en:Association football|Association football]] (soccer). Please do NOT use this stub tag for players of other types of football, e.g. [[Gaelic football]], [[:en:Australian rules football|Australian rules football]], [[rugby union]]. }} ===Tracking categories=== Certain cases of incorrect usage are tracked: *the {{para|newstub}} parameter is blank or absent; *the {{para|category}} parameter is blank or absent. See [[:Category:Stub categories needing attention]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories needing attention}}) Usage of the {{param|message}} parameter is also tracked.
See [[:Category:Stub categories with message parameter]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories with message parameter}}) {{Purge|''Purge this page to update the totals''}} ==== Fixing P-type categories ==== These are categories that lack a 'primary category' that is not a stub category. To fix this, add the primary category to the "category" parameter in the template; this will place the category into the newly specified parent category and remove the category from the "P" subsection in the "Stub categories needing attention" category. Simply adding the new category to the existing list of categories will properly categorize the category, but it will not remove the category from the "attention" category. ==See also== {{other category-header templates}} {{Sandbox other|| {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|| [[ro:Format:CiotCat]] }}}} [[Category:Stub categories| {{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Category namespace templates]] [[Category:WikiProject Stub sorting templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments; consolidate 1 interlanguage link(s) into wikidata link template [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24975162458|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{high-use|15376}} {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|The {{tlx|regional stub category}} template operates identically to {{tlx|stub category}} except that it also categorises the page into {{cl|Stubs by region}}.}} __FORCETOC__ ==Usage== {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article= |newstub= |category= |category type= |WikiProject= |message= }} For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message= }} will show: {{Stub category|article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc.|newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub}} The {{para|category type}} parameter inserts additional text between the phrases "This category is for" and "[[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...", so using {{para|category type|biographical}} will give "This category is for biographical [[Wikipedia:Stub|stub articles]] relating to ...". === Optional parameters === ==== WikiProject ==== {{para|WikiProject}} links to a relevant WikiProject page. For example: {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]] |newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category=Massachusetts |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts }} will show: {{stub category|article=the [[Commonwealth of Massachusetts]]|newstub=Massachusetts-stub |category type=biographical |WikiProject=Massachusetts}} ==== message ==== {{para|message}} adds a message displayed below the main output of the template, but above the table of contents (TOC) which will be automatically generated by {{tl|Automatic category TOC}} if the category contains enough pages. This ensures that the message doesn't get tangled up in the TOC. {{{{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|Regional stub category|Stub category}} |article=people in Japanese [[football (soccer)]], including football players, managers, coaches, etc. |newstub=Japan-footy-bio-stub |category=Japanese footballers |message=This category is only people involved in [[:en:Association football|Association football]] (soccer). Please do NOT use this stub tag for players of other types of football, e.g. [[Gaelic football]], [[:en:Australian rules football|Australian rules football]], [[rugby union]]. }} ===Tracking categories=== Certain cases of incorrect usage are tracked: *the {{para|newstub}} parameter is blank or absent; *the {{para|category}} parameter is blank or absent. See [[:Category:Stub categories needing attention]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories needing attention}}) Usage of the {{param|message}} parameter is also tracked.
See [[:Category:Stub categories with message parameter]] (population: {{PAGESINCATEGORY:Stub categories with message parameter}}) {{Purge|''Purge this page to update the totals''}} ==== Fixing P-type categories ==== These are categories that lack a 'primary category' that is not a stub category. To fix this, add the primary category to the "category" parameter in the template; this will place the category into the newly specified parent category and remove the category from the "P" subsection in the "Stub categories needing attention" category. Simply adding the new category to the existing list of categories will properly categorize the category, but it will not remove the category from the "attention" category. ==See also== {{other category-header templates}} {{Sandbox other|| {{#ifeq:{{{regional|no}}}|yes|| }}}} [[Category:Stub categories| {{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Category namespace templates]] [[Category:WikiProject Stub sorting templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] {{wikidata link||ro|Format:CiotCat}}
